content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of size NNN, construct a simple (i.e, without self-loops and multiedges) **strongly connected directed graph** with NNN nodes where AiA_iAi‚Äã denotes the colour of ithi^{th}ith node.

The graph should satisfy the following properties:


If Ai=AjA_i = A_jAi‚Äã=Aj‚Äã, then nodes iii and jjj should have the same outdegree.
If Ai=AjA_i = A_jAi‚Äã=Aj‚Äã, neither of the edges i‚Üíji \to ji‚Üíj and j‚Üíij \to ij‚Üíi should exist.
If the edge u‚Üívu \to vu‚Üív exists, then the edge v‚Üíuv \to uv‚Üíu cannot exist.
Under the above conditions, the number of edges should be maximized.
**Note:**


It is guaranteed that AAA contains at least 222 distinct elements.
If AAA contains KKK distinct elements, they will be the integers 0,1,2,‚Ä¶,K‚àí10, 1, 2, \ldots, K-10,1,2,‚Ä¶,K‚àí1.
Find any graph that satisfies the above conditions.

If no such graph exists, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test consists of a single integer NNN, denoting the number of nodes in the graph.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, where AiA_iAi‚Äã denotes the colour of iii-th node.




Output Format
For each test case,



If no valid graph exists, output ‚àí1-1‚àí1 on a new line.
Otherwise, first output on a new line MMM: the maximum number of edges in such a graph.
Then, output MMM lines, each containing two space-separated integers uuu and vvv, denoting an edge from uuu to vvv.
constraints: 0‚â§Ai<K0 \leq A_i \lt K0‚â§Ai‚Äã<K, where KKK is the number of distinct elements in AAA.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 100010001000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Nodes 111 and 333 are colored 111, and nodes 222 and 444 are colored 000.
The out degree of nodes [1,3][1,3][1,3] is 111 and out degree of nodes [2,4][2,4][2,4] is also 111, and the graph is strongly connected.
Test case 222: Every node has a distinct color, so those conditions are automatically satisfied. 101010 edges is also the maximum possible number of edges such a graph can have.
